title: Plants SCREAM 😱 too! You just can't hear them #science #physics #plants #indianhistory #india
description:
Plants Can Scream - But We Can't Hear Them | The Untold Story of JC Bose

In 1901, Indian scientist Jagadish Chandra Bose made a discovery that shocked the world: plants respond to stimuli just like animals. They feel pain, recover from fatigue, and even send electrical signals when hurt.

But the British scientific establishment rejected his work for over a decade. Despite inventing wireless communication technology before Marconi and pioneering semiconductor research, Bose faced discrimination and censorship.

This is the story of how one Bengali scientist blurred the line between living and non-living, created the Crescograph machine to detect plant movement, and eventually became known as the Father of Plant Neurobiology.

🔬 Topics Covered:
- JC Bose's revolutionary plant experiments
- The Crescograph invention
- Radio wave demonstrations (1895)
- Colonial discrimination in Indian science
- Plant electrical signals and communication
- Legacy and recognition
